summary: The Assembly will continue public testimony at 5 p.m. Tuesday andalso set a special meeting for Thursday “if needed.”

title: 70 years ago, Henrietta Lacks’s cells were taken without consent. Now, her family wants justice.
summary: On Monday, relatives sued a pharmaceutical company, asking the court to order the firm to relinquish the profit made from Lacks’s cell line.

title: The Alaska Legislature begins 4th session with little hope for a PFD fix
summary: Constitutional amendment proposals lack sufficient support, lawmakers say.

title: Garland says authorities will target school board threats
summary: In a memo, Garland said there has been “a disturbing spike in harassment, intimidation, and threats of violence against school administrators, board members, teachers, and staff who participate in the vital work of running our nation’s public schools.”

title: Biden ends Trump rule barring funds for clinics that advise on abortions
summary: The rule change revokes restrictions that prompted Planned Parenthood clinics to withdraw from the half-century-old family planning program.

title: Gabby Petito and one way to break media’s ‘missing white woman syndrome’
summary: Her story is likely to receive far more media coverage than any other missing-persons case this year, raising the same old skepticism of the implicit racism and classism driving traditional newsrooms’ determination of whose suffering is news and whose suffering isn’t interesting.

title: A parade of snowplows signals the start of winter at JBER
summary: The annual parade draws excited kids (and their F-22 fighter pilot parents)

title: Alaska reports 2nd highest COVID-19 hospitalizations tally and nearly 2,300 cases over the weekend
summary: Some hospitals are reporting that up to nearly half of their patients are COVID-positive, but the situation is fluid and changes from facility to facility and day by day, if not hour by hour, officials said.

summary: Some hospitals are reporting that up to nearly half of their patients are COVID-positive, but the situation is fluid and changes from facility to facility and day by day, if not hour by hour, officials said.

title: 74 homeless people have died outside in Anchorage since 2017. New data shows what’s behind their deaths.
summary: The Daily News used police reports on “outdoor deaths” and autopsy data for a unique analysis. Hypothermia was the top cause of death.

title: Romanian billionaire dies along with family members and others when his private plane crashes near Milan
summary: Dan Petrescu, 68, was a flight enthusiast whose wealth exceeded $3 billion, Italian media reported, including an empire of buildings, hypermarkets and shopping centers.

title: Facebook, WhatsApp, Instagram suffer worldwide outage
summary: The company did not say what might be causing the outage, which began around 11:45 EDT.

title: New Zealand government concedes it can no longer get rid of the coronavirus
summary: Prime Minister Jacinda Ardern announced a cautious plan to ease lockdown restrictions in Auckland, despite an outbreak there that continues to simmer.

title: The mob violence that drove Chinese miners from Juneau in 1886 — and the one man allowed to stay
summary: History: The following is the tale of violence, with a singular exception, that contains a lesson about hatred today.

summary: Six possible legislative maps have been proposed, and one must be approved by Nov. 10.

summary: The man on the bike had died at the scene, and the driver stayed on site and cooperated with the investigation, according to police.

title: With no dam to stop it, the Eklutna River is temporarily reborn. Many hope to keep it alive for good.
summary: Conservation and tribal groups in 2018 removed a downstream dam in river northeast of Anchorage. But an upper-river dam provides the cheapest energy in Southcentral Alaska.

title: U.S. cases ebb after variant burns through the South
summary: A nationwide decline in new infections and hospitalizations is fueled by sharp drops in southern states with low vaccination rates that were hit hard by the highly contagious delta variant.

title: Authorities: Body of missing Florida college student found
summary: A maintenance worker who entered her apartment unauthorized the day she disappeared and later killed himself is the prime suspect.

title: I was young, dumb and got a tattoo of my ex’s name. My current guy can’t seem to get over it.
summary: Relationship advice: My boyfriend says if I was really over my ex, I would have gotten the tat removed. But tattoos are expensive to remove, and time-consuming, and frankly it’s never mattered to me enough.

title: Crisis standards of care are now active for 20 health care facilities across Alaska
summary: Circumstances vary widely for each facility, and Alaskans should still seek hospital care if they need it. “It may be that the resources that they need may not be there,” said the state public health director.

title: Why the fluorescent bill of the crested auklet is like a flashing neon sign
summary: Scientist Hector Douglas has been using brightly painted decoys to study the seabird’s inviting color palette.

title: Are Alaska’s hospitals short-staffed over COVID-19 vaccination mandates? Not yet.
summary: Some health care workers say they plan to leave their jobs or face “voluntary resignation” once vaccine requirements kick in.

title: As coronavirus cases mount and vaccine mandates spread, holdouts plague police and fire departments
summary: Public health experts say the continued reluctance of scores of first responders to be vaccinated poses a particular risk to the public.

title: South Pole posts most severe cold season on record, a surprising anomaly in a warming world
summary: While the rest of the world sizzled, the South Pole shivered with an average temperature of minus-78 degrees over the last six months.

summary: Of the 53 positions cut, 34 are currently vacant and 19 are occupied.

summary: Federal agents had raided the house of a woman in Homer, Alaska, in their search for the missing laptop and the person they suspected of stealing it.

title: Alaska reports over 1,000 COVID cases Friday as ANMC shifts care standards, gets help from Outside workers
summary: At Alaska Native Medical Center, hospital leaders activated crisis standards of care to give staff more flexibility in treating patients. No care has been rationed so far, said the chief medical officer.

summary: Public testimony stretched until midnight and the Assembly did not vote on the ordinance. Another meeting is scheduled for Thursday.

title: Biden addresses Democrats at Capitol as $3.5 trillion government overhaul is scaled back
summary: Negotiations had ground to a halt Friday in Congress despite Speaker Nancy Pelosi’s insistence there would be a vote on a $1 trillion infrastructure bill that is popular but has become snared in the debate over the president’s broader measure.

title: California will enact nation’s first vaccine mandate for schoolchildren
summary: Gov. Gavin Newsom said his administration aims to have all students in seventh through 12th grades vaccinated by next fall.

title: Alaska man killed by metal tool that fell from truck on Massachusetts highway
summary: The man and his wife had just arrived in Massachusetts from Palmer for a vacation when the tool pierced the windshield of their rental car, state police said.

summary: Even with 85% of the country vaccinated, herd immunity remains elusive.

title: Supreme Court Justice Brett Kavanaugh tests positive for COVID, is vaccinated and has no symptoms
summary: The court says Kavanaugh’s wife and daughters are also fully vaccinated, and they tested negative.

title: Merck says experimental pill cuts COVID-19 hospitalizations and deaths by half
summary: The drugmaker said early results showed patients who received molnupiravir within five days of COVID-19 symptoms showed clear benefits. The drug did not benefit patients already hospitalized with severe disease.

title: Testimony over Anchorage mask ordinance to continue for a 4th night Monday
summary: On Thursday, several Assembly members and the mayor called on the public to behave with civility and respect following earlier meetings marked by insults, outbursts and arrests.

title: Surging natural gas prices: Threat to consumers this winter?
summary: Higher costs have coincided with a robust recovery from the pandemic recession, with more homes and businesses burning all forms of fuel.

summary: Like strip malls and the color beige, split-level homes are an unglamorous but quintessential part of Anchorage’s built environment.
